en.title: Red Flag Radar Test
en.subtitle: What's your style when things feel off?
en.description: Everyone handles relationship warning signs differently — what's your approach?

ko.title: 레드플래그 감지력 테스트
ko.subtitle: 관계에서 이상 신호가 올 때 나의 스타일은?
ko.description: 경고 신호를 만났을 때 사람마다 반응이 다르다 — 너는 어떤 타입이야?

en.text:
On a first date, they trash-talk
their ex the entire time

ko.text:
첫 만남에서 상대가
전 연인 욕을 계속한다

en.text: I'd steer the conversation elsewhere — if they circle back, that tells me enough

ko.text: 다른 얘기로 넘기는데 또 돌아오면 그걸로 충분히 파악됐다고 봄

options.rationale: Proactive redirection (Ignite) + pattern recognition test (Prism) + actively protecting own date experience (Self)

en.text: I'd listen carefully — less to the words, more to what they're revealing about themselves

ko.text: 말 내용보다 이 사람이 어떤 사람인지 읽으면서 듣는다

options.rationale: Strategic listening without intervening (Observe) + character analysis (Prism) + independent assessment (Self)

en.text: Breakups hit different for everyone — I'd let them talk it out if they need to

ko.text: 이별은 사람마다 다르니까 필요하면 얘기하게 둔다

options.rationale: Receptive stance (Observe) + empathic understanding of pain (Pulse) + emotional support orientation (Bond)

en.text: At least they're being upfront instead of hiding things — I appreciate the honesty

ko.text: 숨기는 것보다 솔직한 게 낫다고 봐서 오히려 괜찮다

options.rationale: Active positive engagement (Ignite) + intuitive trust response (Pulse) + relationship investment priority (Bond)

en.text:
You're not even official yet
but they say 'You're my everything'

ko.text:
사귀기도 전인데
'나 아니면 안 돼'라고 한다

en.text: I'd be direct — 'that's sweet but let's take this one step at a time'

ko.text: 고마운데 아직 이른 거 아닌가 솔직하게 말한다

options.rationale: Direct verbal pace-setting (Ignite) + rational timing assessment (Prism) + own comfort prioritized (Self)

en.text: Noted — I'd keep watching to see if this is excitement or a pattern

ko.text: 머릿속에 체크해두고 흥분인지 패턴인지 좀 더 지켜본다

options.rationale: Patient monitoring (Observe) + pattern vs noise distinction (Prism) + detached self-centered assessment (Self)

en.text: Some people are just intense when they catch feelings — I'd take it as their way

ko.text: 좋아하면 원래 과한 사람도 있으니까 그 사람의 표현 방식이라고 본다

options.rationale: Accepting stance (Observe) + emotional contextualization (Pulse) + partner understanding orientation (Bond)

en.text: Not gonna lie, the intensity is kind of exciting — I'd see where it goes

ko.text: 솔직히 그 강렬함이 설레기도 해서 어디까지 가나 본다

options.rationale: Active emotional engagement (Ignite) + gut-level excitement response (Pulse) + openness to connection (Bond)

en.text:
You don't reply for an hour
and get dozens of texts back

ko.text:
한 시간 답장 못 했더니
문자가 수십 통 와 있다

en.text: I'd respond once calmly — 'I was busy, but let's talk about the texting thing'

ko.text: 바빴다고 한 번 답하고 '문자 폭탄은 좀 얘기하자'고 한다

options.rationale: Issue-addressing initiative (Ignite) + boundary rationalization (Prism) + own space protection (Self)

en.text: Interesting — I'd note whether this happens again before saying anything

ko.text: 일단 기억해두고 다음에도 이러는지 보고 판단한다

options.rationale: Data-collecting before acting (Observe) + frequency pattern analysis (Prism) + self-paced decision-making (Self)

en.text: They're probably anxious — I'd reassure them first and bring it up gently later

ko.text: 불안해서 그런 거 같아서 일단 안심시키고 나중에 살짝 얘기한다

options.rationale: Gentle approach (Observe) + empathic root-cause intuition (Pulse) + partner reassurance priority (Bond)

en.text: Honestly it's nice knowing someone's thinking about me that much

ko.text: 솔직히 그만큼 내 생각한다는 거니까 나쁘지 않다

options.rationale: Welcoming emotional engagement (Ignite) + feeling-based interpretation (Pulse) + affection-valuing (Bond)

en.text:
They subtly pressure you
to stop seeing your friends

ko.text:
은근히 친구들 안 만났으면
하는 티를 낸다

en.text: I'd name what I'm seeing — 'I've noticed you're not thrilled when I see friends, let's talk'

ko.text: '나 친구 만나는 거 별로인 것 같은데 얘기하자' 직접 꺼낸다

options.rationale: Confronting the observation directly (Ignite) + naming the pattern (Prism) + own social life priority (Self)

en.text: I'd test it — mention plans a few more times and see how they react each time

ko.text: 몇 번 더 약속 얘기를 꺼내보고 반응 패턴을 확인한다

options.rationale: Controlled observation experiment (Observe) + systematic reaction-tracking (Prism) + own social autonomy concern (Self)

en.text: Maybe they're going through something — I'd try to understand what's behind it first

ko.text: 뭔가 이유가 있을 거라 생각하고 왜 그런지 먼저 이해하려 한다

options.rationale: Withholding judgment (Observe) + empathic motive search (Pulse) + partner understanding priority (Bond)

en.text: If they want more time together, I could probably adjust my schedule a bit

ko.text: 같이 있고 싶은 거면 스케줄 좀 맞춰줄 수 있다

options.rationale: Active accommodation (Ignite) + intuitive desire reading (Pulse) + relationship maintenance priority (Bond)

en.text:
When they're upset,
they ghost you for days

ko.text:
화가 나면
며칠째 잠수를 탄다

en.text: When they come back, I'd be upfront — 'disappearing like that doesn't work for me'

ko.text: 돌아오면 바로 말한다 — '그렇게 잠수타는 건 나한테 안 맞아'

options.rationale: Direct confrontation on return (Ignite) + non-negotiable personal standard assertion (Self)

en.text: I'd track how often this happens and whether the pattern is getting worse

ko.text: 얼마나 자주인지 점점 심해지는지 추이를 지켜본다

options.rationale: Longitudinal observation (Observe) + escalation pattern analysis (Prism) + self-directed exit criteria (Self)

en.text: They probably need space to process — I'd send one warm text and wait

ko.text: 혼자 정리할 시간이 필요한 거 같아서 따뜻한 문자 하나 보내고 기다린다

options.rationale: Patient waiting (Observe) + empathic space-giving (Pulse) + partner emotional needs priority (Bond)

en.text: Everyone handles emotions differently — I'd focus on being there when they're ready

ko.text: 감정 처리 방식이 다른 거니까 돌아왔을 때 잘 맞아주는 데 집중한다

options.rationale: Active readiness to reconnect (Ignite) + feeling-based acceptance of difference (Pulse) + relationship repair focus (Bond)

en.text:
They keep posting cozy photos
with other people on social media

ko.text:
SNS에 다른 사람과
다정한 사진을 계속 올린다

en.text: I'd bring it up casually — 'saw your posts, who's that?' — and read their reaction

ko.text: '그 사진 봤는데 누구야?' 가볍게 물어보고 반응을 본다

options.rationale: Proactive but casual inquiry (Ignite) + reaction-reading as data (Prism) + addressing own discomfort (Self)

en.text: I'd check the pattern — is it always the same person? How often? Context matters

ko.text: 매번 같은 사람인지 빈도는 어떤지 맥락을 먼저 파악한다

options.rationale: Silent intelligence-gathering (Observe) + contextual variable analysis (Prism) + self-informed judgment (Self)

en.text: It bugs me but they might just be close friends — I don't want to seem controlling

ko.text: 신경 쓰이지만 친한 친구일 수도 있고 집착처럼 보이기 싫다

options.rationale: Self-suppression to avoid conflict (Observe) + feeling-based benefit of doubt (Pulse) + relationship image protection (Bond)

en.text: They have their own social life and that's healthy — I trust what we have

ko.text: 각자 사회생활이 있는 게 건강한 거고 우리 관계를 믿는다

options.rationale: Positive active framing (Ignite) + intuitive trust in relationship (Pulse) + relationship security investment (Bond)

en.text:
They casually put down
your looks or abilities

ko.text:
외모나 능력을
은근히 깎아내린다

en.text: I'd say it right there — 'that's not funny to me, just so you know'

ko.text: '그건 나한테 웃긴 말이 아니야' 그 자리에서 바로 말한다

options.rationale: Immediate verbal response (Ignite) + self-worth defense (Self) — Process axis neutral in instinctive self-defense

en.text: I'd file it away — if it happens again, then it's a pattern I need to address

ko.text: 기억해두고 또 그러면 그때 패턴으로 잡아서 얘기한다

options.rationale: Controlled non-reaction (Observe) + one-off vs pattern distinction (Prism)

en.text: I'd feel the sting but wonder if they even realized what they said

ko.text: 좀 찔리지만 본인도 모르고 한 말일 수도 있다고 생각한다

options.rationale: Emotional processing of hurt (Pulse) + intent-charitable interpretation for partner (Bond) — Drive axis neutral in internal processing

en.text: I'd laugh it off and dish it back — that's just how some people show closeness

ko.text: 웃으면서 받아치는 편이다 — 그게 친한 사이의 방식이기도 하니까

options.rationale: Playful engagement rather than avoidance (Ignite) + feeling-based reframe (Pulse) + relationship normalizing (Bond)

en.text:
Every time you meet,
money becomes an uncomfortable issue

ko.text:
만날 때마다
돈 문제가 불편해진다

en.text: I'd set a clear arrangement early — 'let's figure out how we handle this'

ko.text: '앞으로 어떻게 할지 정하자'고 일찍 정리한다

options.rationale: Proactive system-building (Ignite) + structural/logical solution (Prism) + setting terms on own comfort (Self)

en.text: I'd start paying attention to the details — who pays what, when, and the vibe around it

ko.text: 누가 언제 얼마나 내는지 그때 분위기가 어떤지 디테일을 챙긴다

options.rationale: Detail-tracking (Observe) + pattern-building from data (Prism) + self-informed assessment (Self)

en.text: Money stuff is always awkward — I'd try to smooth it over so it's less tense

ko.text: 돈 얘기는 원래 불편하니까 내가 분위기 편하게 만들어본다

options.rationale: Tension-absorbing stance (Observe) + emotional comfort instinct (Pulse) + relationship harmony priority (Bond)

en.text: It's just money — I'd rather cover more than let it affect the relationship

ko.text: 돈 때문에 관계가 불편해지느니 내가 좀 더 내는 게 낫다

options.rationale: Active accommodation (Ignite) + values-driven decision over logic (Pulse) + relationship preservation over fairness (Bond)

en.title: I Guard My Lines
en.description:
You know where your boundaries are, and you don't negotiate on them — not for charm, not for chemistry, not for anyone. While others bend their limits hoping it'll all work out, you understood early that protecting your peace isn't selfish, it's survival. You've probably been called "too picky" or "too guarded" by people who didn't like hearing no. You took it as confirmation you're doing something right.

This shows up everywhere: you notice when someone dismisses your feelings as overreacting, you clock when a joke is actually a dig wrapped in a smile, and you know the difference between compromise and self-erasure. You don't need a red flag to be bright red — you catch the beige ones too. The subtle eye-roll when you share something you care about. The way they "forget" things that matter to you but remember everything that matters to them. You file these things away, and your file has a limit.

The strength of this is enormous. Your relationships have a built-in filter that most people wish they had. You attract people who respect structure because you model it. The ones who stay in your life know exactly what they signed up for, and they chose it willingly.

The edge to watch: boundaries can calcify into walls if you're not careful. There's a difference between "I won't tolerate disrespect" and "I leave at the first sign of imperfection." Not every misstep is a red flag — sometimes it's just a human being clumsy with someone they care about. The challenge isn't building the wall. It's knowing when to put in a door.

My peace is non-negotiable.

ko.title: 선 넘으면 바로 끊는다
ko.description:
넌 자기 선이 어디인지 정확히 알고 있고 그 선에 대해서는 협상 안 해. 매력적이든 케미가 좋든 상관없어. 남들이 "이번엔 괜찮겠지" 하면서 기준을 낮출 때 넌 일찍 깨달은 거야 — 내 평화를 지키는 건 이기적인 게 아니라 생존이라는 걸. "너무 까다롭다" 소리 들어본 적 있을 텐데 그걸 오히려 잘하고 있다는 확인으로 받아들였지

이게 일상에서 다 드러나. 네 감정을 "예민하다"고 치부하는 사람 바로 캐치하고 농담으로 포장한 비꼼도 놓치지 않아. 빨간 깃발만 보는 게 아니라 베이지색 깃발도 잡아내지. 네가 중요하게 여기는 건 "까먹으면서" 자기 중요한 건 다 기억하는 사람 — 그런 거 다 파일링 돼 있고 그 파일에는 한도가 있어

이게 엄청난 강점이야. 네 관계에는 대부분이 부러워하는 필터가 내장돼 있거든. 구조를 존중하는 사람들이 네 곁에 남아. 남아있는 사람은 뭘 각오하고 온 건지 정확히 알고 있어

조심할 건 경계가 벽으로 굳을 수 있다는 거야. "무례함은 못 참아"와 "조금이라도 실수하면 끝"은 다른 거거든. 모든 실수가 레드플래그는 아니야. 가끔은 좋아하는 사람 앞에서 서툰 것뿐이야. 벽 쌓는 건 이미 잘해 — 문 만드는 법을 익혀봐

내 평화는 비매품이야

en.title: I Read Between the Lines
en.description:
You don't just hear what people say — you hear what they almost said, what they carefully avoided saying, and what their tone said instead of their words. Your radar doesn't wait for obvious alarms. It picks up frequencies most people don't even know exist. You notice when someone's "I'm fine" has a different texture than last Tuesday's "I'm fine," and you quietly file that away.

This plays out in how you navigate every relationship. You're the friend who says "something felt off about that person" after one dinner and is proven right six months later. You catch when someone mirrors your language a little too perfectly — a sign they're performing rather than connecting. You register micro-expressions, shifts in texting patterns, and the specific way someone changes the subject when a topic makes them uncomfortable. Your subconscious runs pattern recognition software that never sleeps.

This perceptiveness is a genuine gift. You protect yourself and the people you love from situations that look fine on paper but feel wrong in the room. Your instincts have saved you from relationships that would've cost you way more than a few months of loneliness.

The shadow side: being tuned into everything means you can read threats where there are none. A delayed text becomes evidence. A poorly chosen word becomes a character flaw. When your antenna is always up, you exhaust yourself scanning for danger in people who are genuinely just trying their best. Sometimes a weird vibe is just someone having a weird day. Learning to tell the difference between signal and noise is your lifelong project.

I hear what you're not saying.

ko.title: 행간을 읽는다
ko.description:
넌 사람들이 하는 말만 듣는 게 아니야. 하려다 만 말 그리고 조심스럽게 피한 말 그리고 말 대신 톤이 말해주는 것까지 들어. 네 레이더는 뻔한 경보를 기다리지 않아. 대부분이 존재 자체를 모르는 주파수를 잡아내지. 지난 화요일의 "괜찮아"와 오늘의 "괜찮아"의 질감이 다르다는 걸 눈치채고 조용히 저장해두는 타입이야

모든 관계에서 이게 드러나. 한 번 밥 먹고 와서 "저 사람 뭔가 이상해"라고 했는데 6개월 후에 맞는 친구가 바로 너야. 상대가 네 말투를 너무 완벽하게 따라할 때 — 연결이 아니라 연기라는 걸 캐치해. 미세표정이나 답장 패턴 변화나 특정 주제에서 화제를 돌리는 방식 같은 거 다 감지해. 네 무의식은 패턴 인식 프로그램을 24시간 돌리고 있어

이 관찰력이 진짜 선물이야. 서류상으로는 멀쩡한데 방 안에서는 뭔가 잘못된 상황에서 너와 네 주변 사람을 지켜주니까. 네 직감이 몇 달치 외로움보다 훨씬 비싼 대가를 치를 뻔한 관계에서 널 구해준 적 있을 거야

근데 그림자도 있어. 모든 걸 읽다 보면 위협이 아닌 곳에서도 위협을 읽게 돼. 늦은 답장이 증거가 되고 말 한마디 잘못한 게 인격 결함이 돼. 안테나가 항상 켜져 있으면 진짜 최선을 다하는 사람한테서도 위험을 스캔하느라 너만 지쳐. 신호와 소음을 구별하는 게 네 평생 과제야

넌 말 안 해도 들려

en.title: I Feel It Through
en.description:
Your body knows before your brain does. Something shifts in a room — a tone drops half a degree, someone's smile doesn't quite reach their eyes — and you feel it land in your chest before you can name it. You don't analyze red flags. You absorb them. Your emotional compass doesn't work in words; it works in sensations, gut reactions, and a deep knowing that something is either right or very, very wrong.

This means you're the person who gets physically uncomfortable around someone everyone else thinks is great. You can't always explain why — "I just don't feel good around them" isn't the most convincing argument at a dinner party. But you've learned to trust that discomfort because the times you ignored it taught you expensive lessons. You feel tension between two people before either of them has said a word. You sense when affection is performance and when it's real, not through evidence, but through resonance.

Your strength is that you're almost impossible to truly fool. Manipulation works on logic — it gives you reasons to stay, evidence that everything's fine, arguments you can't quite counter. But it struggles against someone whose alarm system bypasses reason entirely. Your body rejects what your mind hasn't caught up to yet, and that's a powerful form of protection.

The challenge: feelings without language can trap you. If you can't articulate why something feels wrong, you might gaslight yourself into staying. "Maybe I'm just anxious. Maybe I'm projecting." Learn to pair that gut reaction with just enough analysis to give it words — not to override it, but to defend it when someone tries to talk you out of your own experience.

My gut hasn't lied yet.

ko.title: 일단 느끼고 본다
ko.description:
네 몸이 머리보다 먼저 알아. 방 안에서 뭔가 달라지면 — 톤이 반 도 내려가거나 누군가의 웃음이 눈까지 안 닿거나 — 이름 붙이기도 전에 가슴에서 먼저 느껴져. 넌 레드플래그를 분석하는 게 아니라 흡수하는 거야. 네 감정 나침반은 말이 아니라 감각과 직감과 "이건 맞다" 혹은 "이건 심각하게 잘못됐다"는 깊은 앎으로 작동해

그래서 넌 남들 다 괜찮다고 하는 사람 옆에서 혼자 몸이 불편한 사람이야. 이유를 설명 못 할 때가 많아 — "그냥 그 사람 옆에 있으면 기분이 안 좋아"가 모임에서 먹히는 논리는 아니니까. 근데 그 불편함을 무시했던 경험이 비싼 수업료를 냈기 때문에 이제는 그 감각을 믿어. 두 사람 사이 긴장감을 말 한마디 없이도 느끼고 애정이 연기인지 진심인지 증거가 아니라 공명으로 구별해

네 강점은 진짜로 속이기 거의 불가능하다는 거야. 조종은 논리에 통하거든 — 머물 이유를 주고 괜찮다는 증거를 대고 반박 못 할 논리를 펼쳐. 근데 경보 시스템이 이성을 아예 우회하는 사람한테는 안 먹혀. 네 몸이 머리보다 먼저 거부하는 거야

근데 말 없는 감정은 함정이 될 수 있어. 왜 불편한지 설명을 못 하면 스스로를 가스라이팅 하게 돼. "내가 예민한 건가" "투영하는 건가" 하면서. 직감에 말을 입혀주는 연습이 필요해 — 직감을 무시하려고가 아니라 누가 흔들 때 방어하려고

내 직감은 아직 틀린 적 없어

en.title: I Lead with Trust
en.description:
You walk into relationships believing people are good until proven otherwise — and you genuinely mean it. Not as a naive default, but as a conscious choice. You've decided that the cost of assuming the worst about everyone is higher than the occasional cost of being wrong about someone. Trust, for you, isn't earned through a series of tests. It's offered as a starting position.

This shapes everything about how you connect. You give people the benefit of the doubt when their text sounds cold. You assume good intentions behind clumsy actions. You're the person who says "maybe they're going through something" when everyone else is ready to write someone off. Your default setting is generosity, and it makes people feel safe enough to actually be honest with you — because they know you won't punish them for being imperfect.

That openness is genuinely rare and genuinely powerful. You create space for real intimacy because people don't have to perform around you. They can show up messy, uncertain, even a little broken, and you'll meet them there without judgment. That's how the deepest connections form — not through testing, but through witnessing.

The blind spot is real, though. Leading with trust means you're sometimes the last to see what everyone else noticed months ago. You extend grace so generously that you might miss the moment grace becomes enabling. Someone's "bad week" becomes their bad month becomes their bad year, and you're still finding reasons to believe. The question isn't whether you should trust less. It's whether you can trust yourself to notice when your trust isn't being honored.

I choose to believe — but I'm not blind.

ko.title: 일단 믿고 본다
ko.description:
넌 관계에 들어갈 때 사람은 기본적으로 좋다고 믿고 시작하는 타입이야. 순진해서가 아니라 의식적으로 그렇게 선택한 거지. 모든 사람을 의심하는 비용이 가끔 한 명한테 속는 비용보다 크다는 걸 깨달은 거야. 신뢰는 시험을 통과해야 얻는 게 아니라 시작점으로 제공하는 거라고 생각해

이게 모든 관계를 만들어. 문자가 차갑게 느껴져도 상대에게 의심의 여지를 줘. 서투른 행동 뒤에 좋은 의도를 먼저 가정해. 다른 사람들이 "걔 그런 애야" 할 때 넌 "뭔가 힘든 일이 있나 봐" 하는 쪽이야. 네 기본 설정이 관대함이라서 사람들은 네 앞에서 솔직해져도 된다고 느껴 — 불완전해도 처벌받지 않을 걸 아니까

이 개방성이 진짜 드물고 진짜 강력해. 사람들이 너한테는 퍼포먼스 안 해도 되니까 진짜 친밀감이 생기는 거야. 지저분하고 불확실하고 좀 부서진 상태로 와도 넌 거기서 판단 없이 만나줘. 가장 깊은 연결은 시험이 아니라 목격에서 만들어지는 거야

근데 사각지대는 확실히 있어. 믿음으로 시작하니까 다른 사람들이 몇 달 전에 본 걸 네가 제일 늦게 보는 경우가 있어. 관용을 너무 넉넉하게 베풀다 보면 그 관용이 방관이 되는 순간을 놓쳐. "힘든 한 주"가 힘든 한 달이 되고 힘든 1년이 되는데 넌 여전히 믿을 이유를 찾고 있거든. 덜 믿으라는 게 아니야 — 네 신뢰가 존중받고 있는지 스스로 알아채는 것이 과제야

믿고 시작해 근데 눈은 뜨고 있어

_designNotes.rewordingScope: all 32 options rewritten to remove correct-answer bias
keyChanges: S options: removed moral superiority language ('my cue to leave', 'clear line')
C options: removed naivete framing ('makes me feel special', 'I let it slide')
All options now read as reasonable responses any person might have
Each type has explicit strengths AND blind spots in result descriptions

axisBalance.process: prism and pulse split evenly across options
axisBalance.compass: self and bond split evenly across options
